prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a tangled jagged scribble of a line, like a stock chart caught in a storm, is lifted by a friendly cartoon hand to reveal it was a neat stack of pure smooth sine waves layered like sheets of colored glass underneath, one of the smaller waves puffing out its chest with comic pride as if it alone matters. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry, knowing, and immediately readable: a reader who just learned this concept should smile in recognition. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A chaotic jagged signal line is peeled back to reveal it is really a tidy stack of smooth pure sine waves layered like colored glass sheets, with one small wave proudly puffing out its chest, illustrating how a complicated time series is a sum of simple oscillations at different frequencies.
caption: Every messy-looking signal is secretly a chord: a stack of pure waves, and the frequency view simply pulls them apart.

prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: several near-identical jittery spiky waveform strips are stacked and pressed together by a gentle cartoon press, and where they overlap the random jitter cancels into a smooth calm curve while the genuine tall peaks reinforce and grow even more solid and bright. alt: Several jittery spiky waveform strips are pressed together so the random noise cancels into a smooth calm floor while the genuine tall peaks reinforce and brighten, illustrating how averaging overlapping segments turns the inconsistent periodogram into a trustworthy consistent spectral estimate.
caption: Average many noisy spectra and the random jitter cancels while the true peaks reinforce: that is how an inconsistent estimate becomes one you can trust.

prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cartoon character holds an adjustable zoom lens over a drifting signal, and a clear visual seesaw or balance scale shows the trade: zoomed in for sharp time the lens sees a precise instant but a blurry smear of frequency, zoomed out for sharp frequency it sees crisp pitch but a smeared vague moment, the two views shown side by side as the lens dial turns. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry, knowing, and immediately readable: a reader who just learned this concept should smile in recognition. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A character turns an adjustable zoom lens over a drifting signal while a balance seesaw shows the trade between a sharp instant with blurry frequency and a crisp pitch with a smeared moment, illustrating how wavelets trade time resolution against frequency resolution across scales.
caption: Wavelets are a zoom lens with a catch: sharpen time and frequency blurs, sharpen frequency and the moment smears. You cannot have both at once.

after_heading: The Short-Time Fourier Transform and the Spectrogram
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single Fourier spectrum, drawn as one confident steady glowing horizontal bar, smugly insists a rhythm is perfectly constant, while behind it a time-aware spectrogram strip reveals the same band actually flickering bright then dim then drifting like a faulty neon tube, exposing the steady bar as overconfident and wrong. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry, knowing, and immediately readable: a reader who just learned this concept should smile in recognition. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A single confident steady glowing bar from a Fourier spectrum insists a rhythm is constant, while a time-aware spectrogram strip behind it shows the same band actually flickering bright and dim and drifting like a faulty neon tube, illustrating how a single spectrum hides a cycle that strengthens and fades over time.
caption: One spectrum swears the cycle never changes; the spectrogram quietly shows it flickering with the work week. Time-localization catches what a single spectrum hides.
